Deanna, I am writing to reiterate that all communication between us must remain consistent with the Agreed Final Decree of Divorce (Cause No. 18-1566-FC3) and the Agreed Order in Suit to Modify Parent- Child Relationship (Cause No. 18-2085-FC4). Both court orders require that all communication remain respectful, limited to child-related matters, and conducted in a way that promotes the emotional stability and well-being of our children. Over the past several weeks, your messages and emails have included multiple comments that are inconsistent with those requirements. For example: • Referring to me as a “liar and a coward” and stating that I am “stealing from us again. ” • Stating that Jesica is “a stranger, ” “awkward, ” and “should never have been involved. ” • Writing that you “can’t afford to feed Kass or yourself until [my] wife decides it’s ok. ” • Telling the children that I am responsible for your financial hardship and uncertainty about their housing or schooling. These types of comments violate the Permanent Injunction provisions (Items 2–5 and 10) of the June 6, 2023 Modification Order and contradict the directive under Texas Family Code §153.001 that both parents foster a stable, non-hostile, and supportive environment for the children. Please refrain from making emotional or derogatory remarks about me, my spouse, or our home— especially in front of the children or within their hearing. These statements create confusion, distress, and instability for them. The children must not be used as messengers or intermediaries in adult conflicts, nor should they be told or encouraged to believe that one parent is at fault for the family’s financial or personal circumstances. For clarity, Jesica is not a stranger to our family. She and I have known each other for nearly 30 years, and she visited our home in Star Ranch multiple times between 2010 and 2012. We are now legally married, and she is an established, permanent part of my life. Jesica has every right to be involved in communications concerning the children’s schedules, transportation, and any fiduciary responsibilities that fall outside the existing court orders. Her involvement is lawful, appropriate, and solely focused on supporting the children’s stability and welfare. I will continue to meet all obligations under the current court orders and maintain a structured, positive environment for the children. If you believe a modification or clarification is needed regarding financial or custodial matters, please address it through the Office of the Attorney General or by filing a formal motion with the Williamson County Court. All future correspondence should remain factual, respectful, and limited to matters directly related to the children’s well-being or logistics. Communication that includes personal attacks, accusations, or disparagement will not receive a response and will be documented for the record. Jeremiah  ↪

This notice is to document and formally object to interference with my possession and access to our daughter, Kassidy Grace Cargill, under the terms of the Agreed Order in Suit to Modify Parent– Child Relationship (Cause No. 18-1566-FC3, Williamson County District Court). Today, I was informed by our children that Kassidy did not attend school and that she was allegedly being kept at home under the care of her grandmother. As today is my court-ordered day of possession, and no communication or notice of illness has been provided, your actions constitute an interference with my lawful possession and access rights. Pursuant to the standing order and Texas Family Code §157.001, both parties are required to comply with the possession schedule and to refrain from withholding or concealing a child during the other parent’s designated period of possession. Any such interference may be considered contempt of court and can result in enforcement or modification proceedings. I respectfully request immediate written confirmation of Kassidy’s location and assurance that she will be released into my possession as required by the current court order. If she is ill, please provide reasonable documentation and proposed arrangements to ensure continued compliance with the possession schedule. Failure to comply or continued concealment will result in this correspondence being submitted as evidence in the pending TRO and modification filings. Respectfully, Jeremiah Thomas Cargill 520 Wolf Ranch Pkwy Georgetown, TX 78626 (512) 676-0060 Email: [your preferred email]

NOTICE OF UNLAWFUL SELF‑HELP EVICTION AND DEMAND TO PRESERVE PROPERTY Date: November 04, 2025 To: Deanna Helene Cargill Via text/email (and preserved for the record) From: Jeremiah Thomas Cargill 805 Muster Bend, Georgetown, TX 78626 Phone: (512) 676-0060 | Email: jeremiah@example.com Re: Occupancy Rights, Access, and Preservation of Personal Property This letter is to place you on formal notice that I have legally occupied the shared residence since June 1, 2025, with your consent and while contributing approximately half of the rent and household expenses each month. Under Texas law, this establishes a residential tenancy by implication, and I am not a trespasser. Legal Basis (Texas Law) • Texas Property Code § 24.005 requires written notice and a court proceeding (forcible detainer/ eviction) before a residential occupant can be required to vacate. Absent a court order, any attempt to remove me or my belongings is unlawful. • Texas Rules of Civil Procedure and Property Code § 24.0061 provide that only a peace officer acting under a valid writ of possession may remove an occupant or property following a judgment. Self‑help eviction (changing locks, discarding property, denying access) is not permitted. • Texas Property Code Chapter 24A (Writ of Retrieval) expressly addresses retrieval of personal property from a former residence and confirms that removal of another’s property without lawful process can expose the actor to civil and criminal liability. Demand and Preservation 1) Do not dispose of, move, sell, give away, or otherwise interfere with any of my personal property (including the Mini Cooper, computers/servers, clothing, tools, and documents). Preserve all items in their current condition. 2) Do not deny me reasonable access to retrieve my property. If access coordination is needed, I will schedule a civil standby with local law enforcement so retrieval can occur safely and peacefully. 3) Do not change locks, disable garage access, or otherwise exclude me without a court order. Any such actions will be treated as an unlawful exclusion and conversion of property. 4) If you believe I must vacate, you must pursue the proper legal process through written notice and an eviction filing; I will comply with any lawful court order. Proposed Coordination Window Please propose two (2) dates and times within the next seven (7) days for me to retrieve specified items in an orderly manner. I am willing to coordinate a civil standby with Georgetown Police to ensure a smooth transaction. Reservation of Rights Nothing in this notice waives any rights or remedies. If my property is removed, destroyed, or withheld, I will seek immediate relief, including a Writ of Retrieval, and pursue civil claims for conversion and damages, and if appropriate, criminal reporting. Sincerely, Jeremiah Thomas Cargill

Deanna, This letter serves as formal notice that I dispute the validity and enforceability of the “Notice to Vacate (Illegal Activity)” dated November 6, 2025. The document you issued does not meet the legal requirements set forth under the Texas Property Code §24.005 and §92.0081, which govern notice to vacate and tenant removal procedures. Specifically: 1. You are not the landlord or property owner, and I am not a subtenant under any written lease with you; therefore, you lack standing to issue a lawful eviction notice. 2. Even if such notice were permissible, Texas law prohibits “self-help” evictions. You cannot order me to vacate, remove my belongings, or deny access to the property without a court-issued judgment for possession from the appropriate Justice of the Peace Court in Williamson County. 3. The allegations contained in your notice are unsubstantiated and defamatory. You have not presented any verified evidence of “illegal activity, ” and making false criminal accusations in writing constitutes libel per se under Texas law. 4. I have maintained partial rent and bill payments for shared occupancy of the residence since June 2025, establishing tenant-at-will status under Texas law. You cannot lawfully terminate my possession without proper judicial process. Any attempt to: • remove or dispose of my personal property, • deny me access to the premises, • or otherwise interfere with my legal occupancy rights will constitute an unlawful lockout and violation of Texas Property Code §92.0081(b). If you believe you have legitimate cause for eviction, you must file a petition for eviction through the Williamson County Justice Court. Until such time, I will consider any self-help actions or interference with my property or access as unlawful and will respond accordingly through legal and law enforcement channels. Sincerely, Jeremiah Thomas Cargill 805 Muster Bend, Georgeto
